findings.message: No version constraint on `requests`. Every other production dependency carries a lower-bound pin (e.g. `>=2.32`). An unpinned transitive pull can silently install an older or future-incompatible release and weakens reproducibility.

`httpx>=0.28` is already declared in `[dependency-groups] dev`. The project uses FastAPI (async); `httpx` is the idiomatic async-native HTTP client for this stack and is already a known-good dep. Adding the synchronous `requests` to production deps introduces a second HTTP client library without an apparent justification in the diff.
findings.suggestion: If an HTTP client is needed in production code, promote `httpx` from the dev group to the main `dependencies` list instead of adding `requests`. If `requests` is genuinely required (e.g. by a third-party SDK that imports it directly), document that reason in a comment and pin the version.

findings.message: `requests` is a blocking/synchronous library. Called directly inside an `async def` FastAPI route or background task it will block the event loop, degrading throughput under load.
findings.suggestion: Use `httpx.AsyncClient` for async call sites, or run `requests` calls in a thread pool via `asyncio.to_thread()` / `run_in_executor()` if `requests` must be retained.

findings.message: The diff adds only the dependency declaration; no new source file using `requests` is included. Without a corresponding usage site it is unclear what need this covers and whether it duplicates `httpx`.
findings.suggestion: Confirm the call site exists and cannot be served by the already-present `httpx` before merging.
